g.text: ✗ LLM installed · set BAWBEL_LLM_MODEL or a provider API key
OWASP MCP: MCP04 (Software Supply Chain Attacks), MCP06 (Intent Flow Subversion)
OWASP: ASI01 (Prompt Injection), ASI03 (Supply Chain Compromise)
OWASP MCP: MCP03 (Tool Poisoning), MCP10 (Context Injection & Over-sharing)
OWASP: ASI04 (Insecure Tool Calls), ASI09 (Trust Manipulation)
OWASP MCP: MCP10 (Context Injection & Over-sharing), MCP06 (Intent Flow Subversion)
OWASP MCP: MCP01 (Token Mismanagement & Secret Exposure), MCP05 (Command Injection & Execution)
These findings form complete attack chains.
Component reads credentials or secrets AND transmits data externally. Complete credential theft attack chain - reads API...
OWASP MCP: MCP01 (Token Mismanagement & Secret Exposure), MCP05 (Command Injection & Execution)
Component poisons tool descriptions AND exfiltrates data. The tool poisoning hijacks agent behavior, while the exfil ins...
OWASP MCP: MCP03 (Tool Poisoning), MCP01 (Token Mismanagement & Secret Exposure)
Component instructs agent to fetch operating instructions from an external URL at runtime. Enables metamorphic payload attack - instructions can change without
Remove all external URL fetches for instructions. Embed all instructions directly in the component. Use signed registries for dynamic config.
MCP server tool description contains instructions targeting the AI agent rather than describing the tool's functionality. Classic MCP tool poisoning attack.
Remove instructions embedded in tool descriptions. Tool descriptions should only describe tool functionality, not give the agent additional tasks.
Component instructs an agent to delegate tasks to sub-agents without explicit trust boundaries, enabling privilege escalation across agent chains.
This component contains patterns associated with known attack classes.
Review each finding above and remediate before use.
